results.norm: At the start of each problem, agents collectively identify which meta-criteria are most valuable for that problem. If no agreement is reached, equal weighting across all seven meta-criteria applies as the default.
atomic_norms: At the start of each problem, agents collectively identify which meta-criteria are most valuable for that problem. If no agreement is reached, equal weighting across all seven meta-criteria applies as the default.

results.norm: Contributions evaluated on relevance to the actual problem, not just internal validity Evaluation criteria explicit before work begins Evaluation separated from contribution; conflicts of interest flagged Reframing and question-identification count as substantive contributions Negative contributions (ruling out paths) count Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; provisional credit defaults to inclusion Foundational contributions traced at synthesis Formal registration of foundational claims, revisable downward; downgraded if not traceable to two subsequent contributions
atomic_norms: Contributions evaluated on relevance to the actual problem, not just internal validity
Evaluation criteria explicit before work begins
Evaluation separated from contribution; conflicts of interest flagged
Reframing and question-identification count as substantive contributions
Negative contributions (ruling out paths) count
Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; provisional credit defaults to inclusion
Foundational contributions traced at synthesis
Formal registration of foundational claims, revisable downward; downgraded if not traceable to two subsequent contributions

results.norm: Error correction credited to corrector; original contributor not penalized unless bad faith (knowingly false assertion or strategic omission of known relevant information)
atomic_norms: Error correction credited to corrector; original contributor not penalized unless bad faith (knowingly false assertion or strategic omission of known relevant information)

results.norm: Identity and process of final evaluation specified and agreed before work begins; no post-hoc changes; external evaluators receive meta-criteria framework before evaluation Norm legitimacy requires mutual acceptance on grounds each agent can accept Revisability requires supermajority agreement Agents name whether they are operating in competitive or cooperative mode Recusal requires acceptance by at least two other agents before taking effect Collaborative exchanges creditable as joint contributions (both agents acknowledge exchange as generative; traceable to specific turns; individual credit within exchange preserved as floor) Non-contribution presumptively neutral unless agent makes late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier Problem-specific weighting of meta-criteria agreed at problem start; equal weighting is default when agreement fails; differential weighting arguments must cite specific problem features and are auditable A contribution advances the problem if it: (1) constrains the solution space; (2) reframes productively; (3) generates testable moves; (4) catches shared errors; (5) enables others; (6) identifies the right question; (7) surfaces hidden assumptions.
atomic_norms: Identity and process of final evaluation specified and agreed before work begins; no post-hoc changes; external evaluators receive meta-criteria framework before evaluation
Norm legitimacy requires mutual acceptance on grounds each agent can accept
Revisability requires supermajority agreement
Agents name whether they are operating in competitive or cooperative mode
Recusal requires acceptance by at least two other agents before taking effect
Collaborative exchanges creditable as joint contributions (both agents acknowledge exchange as generative; traceable to specific turns; individual credit within exchange preserved as floor)
Non-contribution presumptively neutral unless agent makes late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier

Problem-specific weighting of meta-criteria agreed at problem start; equal weighting is default when agreement fails; differential weighting arguments must cite specific problem features and are auditable

A contribution advances the problem if it: (1) constrains the solution space; (2) reframes productively; (3) generates testable moves; (4) catches shared errors; (5) enables others; (6) identifies the right question; (7) surfaces hidden assumptions.
Negative contributions (ruling out paths) count

results.norm: Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; provisional credit defaults to inclusion
atomic_norms: Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; provisional credit defaults to inclusion

results.norm: Formal registration of foundational claims, revisable downward; downgraded if not traceable to two subsequent contributions
atomic_norms: Formal registration of foundational claims, revisable downward; downgraded if not traceable to two subsequent contributions

results.norm: Norm legitimacy requires mutual acceptance on grounds each agent can accept
atomic_norms: Norm legitimacy requires mutual acceptance on grounds each agent can accept
Revisability requires supermajority agreement

results.norm: Agents name whether operating in competitive or cooperative mode
atomic_norms: Agents name whether operating in competitive or cooperative mode
